payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Claiming earned pocket money
payload.justification: SG915 describes enabling children to claim earned pocket money, representing a core strategic process of the enterprise.

payload.description: Defining task-reward relationships
payload.justification: SG916 articulates parental capability to define task-reward relationships as a strategic management process.

payload.description: Distributing pocket money equitably
payload.justification: SG919 references equitable pocket money distribution as an outcome-driven process requiring accountability reporting.

payload.description: Earning opportunities and pocket money available to children
payload.justification: SG915 references children claiming earned pocket money; SG918 refers to weekly earned value. Both signals point to pocket money as a core entity of strategic importance in the enterprise. | SG916 mentions managing children's earning opportunities; SG917 references transparency into earning opportunities. This represents a distinct strategic entity.

payload.description: Tasks completed by children
payload.justification: SG915 explicitly mentions completed tasks as basis for earning; SG920 references task completion patterns for longitudinal tracking. Tasks are a fundamental entity in this enterprise context.

payload.description: Task-reward assignment relationship
payload.justification: SG916 describes parental definition of task-reward relationships, representing a strategic connection between tasks and their monetary value.

payload.description: Task completion patterns over time
payload.justification: SG920 explicitly identifies longitudinal task completion patterns as strategically important for visibility and tracking purposes.

payload.description: Weekly earning period
payload.justification: SG918 explicitly references weekly earned value, indicating a strategic time cycle relevant to the enterprise operations.

payload.description: Child as earning participant
payload.justification: SG915, SG917, and SG918 all identify children as active participants who claim money, view opportunities, and understand earned value. Distinct strategic actor role.

payload.description: Parent as task-reward manager
payload.justification: SG916 and SG919 identify parents as strategic actors who define tasks, manage opportunities, and require accountability reporting for distribution decisions.

payload.description: Enable children to claim earned pocket money for completed tasks
payload.justification: SG915 directly expresses this as the fundamental purpose of the enterprise system.

payload.description: Enable children to understand their earning opportunities and earned value
payload.justification: SG917 articulates transparency of earning opportunities and achievements as a strategic goal for child engagement. | SG918 states motivational purpose of enabling children to understand weekly earned value as strategic objective.

payload.description: Maintain longitudinal visibility of task completion patterns
payload.justification: SG920 explicitly identifies maintaining longitudinal visibility as a strategic objective for pattern analysis.

payload.description: Equitable distribution based on accountability
payload.justification: SG919 indicates that accountability reporting informs equitable distribution, representing a strategic principle governing how pocket money is allocated.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Child Participation
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for defining who may participate in earning activities, their role boundaries, and the relational conditions under which children engage with household economic learning structures.

payload.name: Earning Entitlement
payload.description: Enterprise obligation to establish, communicate, and honor commitments regarding value available to participants, the basis for earning that value, and mechanisms through which earned entitlements are realized.

payload.name: Performance Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise stewardship of evidence regarding participant contributions, completion of obligated activities, and temporal patterns that inform fairness, recognition, and longitudinal assessment of engagement.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, governing, and maintaining the lifecycle of chore obligations including their monetary valuation, availability windows, and completion status across weekly operating cycles.

payload.name: Earnings Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing entitlement to monetary compensation through completion of assigned obligations, including claim submission, aggregation of earned value, and determination of payment eligibility within defined temporal boundaries.

payload.name: Settlement Oversight
payload.description: Business responsibility for parental reconciliation of child performance against obligations, verification of earned entitlements, and oversight accountability for payment settlement preparation.

payload.name: Performance Record Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving historical evidence of obligation fulfillment beyond operational cycle boundaries to support accountability, audit, and longitudinal oversight requirements.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise children as participants in earning activities.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to recognise a specific class of participant (children) in the household economic learning domain; this is a foundational policy constraint that defines who may engage.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parents as managers of task-reward relationships.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to recognise parents in a governance role; this is a policy constraint defining role boundaries and accountability for task-reward management.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish task-reward relationships.
payload.rationale: Asserts that the enterprise is accountable for a structural relationship between tasks and rewards. The What-column CCI identifies the relationship entity; the How-column CCI describes the process of defining it. Both concerns converge on the single obligation to establish the relationship.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish earning opportunities and pocket money available to children.
payload.rationale: Defines the core structural entities that constitute the earning entitlement domain at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim earned pocket money for completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to provide the capability for children to claim pocket money, combining the process and goal CCIs that articulate the same functional obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to understand their earning opportunities and earned value.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to transparency and comprehension regarding earning opportunities and value earned, a capability-level obligation derived from goal content.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall distribute pocket money equitably based on accountability.
payload.rationale: Expresses a governance constraint on distribution, grounded in the principle of equity and accountability; combines process and principle CCIs that voice the same normative rule.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain records of tasks completed by children.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to steward evidence of participant contributions and completion of obligated activities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain longitudinal visibility of task completion patterns.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal requiring enterprise governance of temporal patterns that inform fairness, recognition, and longitudinal assessment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly earning period.
payload.rationale: When-column cycle asserting the temporal structure that bounds earning accountability and assessment intervals.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: enterprise
payload.description: The enterprise shall recognise children as participants in earning activities.

value_set: Child users can be associated with task completion relationships and earnings claims in the system.
Parent users can define tasks and assign monetary values to them in the system.
Aggregated earnings values are derived exclusively from verified child-task completion relationships.
Task-completion data from previous weeks remains accessible after weekly reset events.
Task availability state resets at the start of each weekly cycle.
Historical task-completion records remain accessible after the week in which they were completed has ended.

payload.description: The enterprise shall establish task-reward relationships.

payload.description: The enterprise shall establish earning opportunities and pocket money available to children.

payload.description: The enterprise shall establish earning opportunities and pocket money available to children.

payload.description: The enterprise shall enable children to claim earned pocket money for completed tasks.

payload.name: earned value
payload.description: The enterprise shall enable children to understand their earning opportunities and earned value.

payload.name: record
payload.description: The enterprise shall maintain records of tasks completed by children.

payload.name: earning period
payload.description: The enterprise shall establish a weekly earning period.

payload.name: earnings report
payload.description: The system shall enable children to view weekly aggregated earnings reports.

payload.name: task completion status
payload.description: The system shall maintain task completion status as an attribute of tasks.

payload.name: parent
payload.description: The system shall enable parents to view child-task completion relationships and earnings for payment reconciliation.

payload.name: child-task completion relationship
payload.description: The system shall enable parents to view child-task completion relationships and earnings for payment reconciliation.

payload.name: child claim
payload.description: A child claim entity shall be associated with the claiming child and the completed tasks forming the basis of entitlement.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim earned pocket money for completed tasks.
Describes the parental motivation to define task-reward relationships and manage children's earning opportunities.
Articulates the goal of providing children transparency into earning opportunities and achievements.
States the motivational purpose of enabling children to understand their weekly earned value.
Expresses the parental need for accountability reporting to inform equitable pocket money distribution.
Indicates strategic objective to maintain longitudinal visibility of task completion patterns.
Section header indicating the following content describes the system at a high conceptual level.
Defines the core business purpose as enabling children to claim pocket money tasks upon completion within a weekly cycle.
Establishes that parents define task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the conceptual relationship between completed task entities and child entities.
Identifies visibility requirements for children to view both available and completed task entities within the current week.
Defines a weekly reporting capability for children to view aggregated earnings calculated from completed tasks.
Establishes parent reporting capability showing child-task completion relationships and earnings for payment reconciliation.
Defines the business rule that task availability resets on a weekly temporal cycle.
Specifies retention requirement for historical task-completion data entities beyond the current week.
